_meta.source: Research-sourced context figures — every value carries its own citation. Compiled from research/us-national-debt-2026-07 findings (holders.md, history-and-outlook.md).
_meta.rule: No value here without a named source + as-of date. See substrate research project for full citations.

intragovFunds.note: Largest Government Account Series holders; remainder computed at build as 'Other federal accounts'.
intragovFunds.asof: SSA/CMS 2026 Trustees (end-2025); DoD FY2024 audited AFR; CRS/OPM FY2024

funds.name: Military Retirement Fund
funds.source: DoD FY2024 MRF Agency Financial Report

domesticPrivate.note: Category proportions from Fed Z.1 L.210 / PGPF (2024-2025), scaled at build time to the current domestic-private residual (public debt minus Fed minus foreign). 'Individuals, money-market funds & other' is the within-category remainder.

outlays.source: CRFB 'Interest Costs Just Surpassed Defense and Medicare'; Taxpayers for Common Sense (FY2025 net interest ~$970B vs defense ~$917B)

population.source: US Census Bureau resident population (~342.4M, 2025); census.gov/popclock

chinaPeak.source: TIC via SCMP/Global Times — peak ~$1.32T Nov 2013

projections.edition: CBO, The Long-Term Budget Outlook: 2025 to 2055 (March 2025)
projections.measure: debt held by the public, % of GDP
projections.altScenarioNote: extending expiring policies (CRFB analysis of CBO alternative scenario)

foreignContext.officialShareNote: TIC SLT Table 5, May 2026: $3,848.0B official of $9,371.1B total = 41.1% official / 58.9% private
foreignContext.setserCaveat: Custody-adjusted (Euroclear/Belgium etc.), China's true US exposure est. $1.8-1.9T — Brad Setser, CFR 'Finding China in the U.S. TIC Data'

ratings.reason: rising debt & interest costs; deficits unaddressed. Last top-tier rating lost (held since 1917)

interestContext.source: Econofact interest-burden explainer; Treasury avg-rates dataset
